# CHAPTER 2

## REVIEW QUESTIONS

Completion. Fill in the blanks with the appropriate term from the chapter.

1. The four most abundant chemical $\_\_\_\_$ in living matter are carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen.
2. Amino acids and proteins all contain the element $\_\_\_\_$ in addition to carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.
3. Compounds based on a carbon backbone are referred to as $\_\_\_\_$.
4. The weak attractions that hold water molecules together are called $\_\_\_\_$.
5. The most abundant inorganic compound in living cells is $\_\_\_\_$.
6. Plants and cyanobacteria capture light energy and store it as chemical energy by means of $\_\_\_\_$.
7. The six-carbon sugar that cells most commonly use as an energy source is $\_\_\_\_$.
8. Fats, oils, and waxes are all examples of $\_\_\_\_$.
9. Joining two monomers with the loss of a water molecule is called $\_\_\_\_$.
10. Proteins are linear polymers built from monomers called $\_\_\_\_$.
11. The covalent bond between two amino acids in a protein is the $\_\_\_\_$.
12. Biological catalysts that lower the activation energy of a reaction are called $\_\_\_\_$.
13. The polymer that stores genetic information in cells is $\_\_\_\_$.
14. The five-carbon sugar found in RNA but not in DNA is $\_\_\_\_$.
15. In RNA, the nitrogenous base $\_\_\_\_$ replaces the thymine that is found in DNA.

## Answers to Review Questions

## Chapter 2

## Completion
1. elements 2. nitrogen 3. organic compounds 4 . hydrogen bonds 5 . water 6 . photosynthesis 7. glucose 8. lipids 9. dehydration synthesis 10. amino acids 11. peptide bond 12. enzymes 13. deoxyribonucleic acid 14. ribose 15. uracil
